verdict.answer_first: A states the answer immediately: "Big-O describes how an algorithm's cost grows as its input grows"; B also answers first with the same core definition but adds more framing before examples.
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive because it lands on one concrete practical recommendation: "nested loop to set/dict lookup" as the most common optimization; B surveys more cases and ends with a follow-up prompt.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the main trap and fix in a compact sequence: O(n²) duplicate check, then O(n) set version, then the practical takeaway.
